title: Sibling boundary + shared access-tier vocabulary / schema-contract doc
context: Four Hee-Lee Oss cancer projects risk overlapping: open-cohort-catalog (breadth/access-tier layer), cancer-dataset-datasheets (depth/narrative), ewing-open-data-catalog (disease vertical), and cancer-data-dictionaries (variable-level). To avoid four overlapping cancer-data catalogs, this project is intended to be the canonical owner of the shared access-tier vocabulary and schema contract that the siblings consume. Without a written, contractual boundary document, this split is merely implied and divergence into four tier taxonomies is likely. This task depends on schema-001 for the access-tier taxonomy to be defined before it can be documented as the shared contract.
objective: Write and commit the sibling boundary document that defines each project's scope, designates open-cohort-catalog as the canonical owner of the access-tier vocabulary + schema contract, specifies the shared enum and record-id reference that siblings consume, and prevents taxonomic divergence across the four Hee-Lee Oss cancer projects.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed boundary document (e.g. SIBLINGS.md or a section in PLAN.md) explicitly defines the role of each sibling project: open-cohort-catalog = breadth/access-tier+license layer; cancer-dataset-datasheets = depth (references this project's record by id); ewing-open-data-catalog = disease vertical (consumes this schema/vocabulary); cancer-data-dictionaries = variable-level (links up to cohort records).

The document defines the shared access-tier enum (OPEN | OPEN-AGGREGATE | REGISTERED-OPEN | NON-COMMERCIAL | CONTROLLED) and the record-id reference contract that siblings reuse, with an explicit rule that siblings consume rather than re-derive the vocabulary.
The document states that siblings must not invent their own access-tier taxonomy or schema for fields this project owns; divergence resolution is escalated to this project's maintainer.
The document defines the shared schema-contract artifact (package name, versioning policy, or pointer to the schema file) that siblings import.
output: A committed sibling boundary document defining the written, contractual split between the four Hee-Lee Oss cancer projects and specifying the shared access-tier vocabulary + schema-contract package that siblings consume, preventing taxonomic divergence.
